title: Spy TV
subtitle: Espionage and political-thriller TV. Le Carré tradition, modern surveillance, international.

items.title: The Night Manager
items.pitch: BBC/AMC. Le Carré adaptation. Hiddleston, Hugh Laurie, Olivia Colman, Tom Hollander. The arms-dealer-infiltration plot is exactly your espionage lane.

critics.quote: Won 3 Globes including Hiddleston's Lead Actor. Critically acclaimed.

sections.name: II. High Priority
sections.desc: Strongest probable fits given your Tinker Tailor / Bourne / Hunt for Red October / Spy Game / All The President's Men loves.

items.title: The Bureau (Le Bureau des Légendes)
items.whyPriority: Strongest single recommendation across this entire build. French DGSE drama. Slow, methodical, Le-Carré-tier intelligence work. Considered by many critics the greatest spy show ever made. Direct fit.
items.pitch: DGSE intelligence officers run deep-cover legends abroad. The protagonist returns from six years undercover in Damascus and can't stop being someone he isn't.

critics.quote: Routinely cited among the greatest TV dramas of the 2010s. Critical consensus places it as the gold-standard spy drama.

items.title: Tinker Tailor Soldier Spy (BBC, 1979)
items.whyPriority: Alec Guinness as George Smiley. Considered by Le Carré devotees as superior to the 2011 Oldman film because the length allows the full novel to breathe. ~5 hours total.
items.pitch: BBC's faithful adaptation. The mole-hunt unfolds at the pace Le Carré wrote it.

critics.quote: Considered the gold-standard Le Carré adaptation by most readers of the books.

items.title: Smiley's People (BBC, 1982)
items.whyPriority: The Tinker Tailor sequel. Guinness returns as Smiley. Closes out the Karla trilogy on TV. Direct continuation.
items.pitch: Smiley is brought out of retirement to investigate a Russian general's death. Guinness's last great Smiley work.

critics.quote: Critical equal to Tinker Tailor. Considered an essential pair.

items.title: Slow Horses
items.seasons: S1-S5 aired; renewed through S7. Each season ~6 episodes.
items.whyPriority: Apple TV+. Mick Herron's Slough House novels. Gary Oldman as Jackson Lamb leading MI5's washed-up agents. Critically beloved, structurally tight, six-episode seasons that play like films.
items.pitch: MI5 keeps its failures in a department called Slough House. Lamb is their disgusting, brilliant boss. Each season is one operation.

critics.quote: Routinely cited among the best ongoing dramas. Already considered the best contemporary spy show on TV alongside The Bureau.

items.title: The Americans
items.whyPriority: Cross-listed Drama TV. KGB sleeper-agent couple in 1980s suburban DC. Spy mechanics drive a marriage drama. Final season among TV's great closures.
items.pitch: Two KGB officers planted as a married American couple in Reagan-era suburbs. Their FBI counterintelligence neighbor doesn't know.

critics.quote: Won Outstanding Drama for final season. Russell and Rhys won Lead Actress and Actor.

items.title: Patriot
items.whyPriority: Amazon. Cult masterpiece. Tonally singular — folk-music-driven dark-comic existential spy drama. Tragic that it was cancelled. A direct match for your Coen sensibility plus espionage.
items.pitch: An undercover State Department officer takes a job at a Milwaukee industrial piping firm so he can launder money to influence an Iranian election. He also writes folk songs about his cover work.

critics.quote: Critical favorite. Cancelled despite glowing reviews; one of the most heralded under-watched shows of its era.

sections.name: III. Medium Priority
sections.desc: Strong second-tier fits and adjacent territory.

items.title: The Sandbaggers
items.whyPriority: British MI6 drama. Cited as a key influence on The Wire and on Le Carré-tradition spy fiction. Procedural, dialogue-driven, almost no action.
items.pitch: London MI6 desk officers manage agents in the field. The viewer almost never sees the field.

critics.quote: Cult classic. David Mamet has cited it as among his favorite TV dramas. Underseen because difficult to find for years.

items.title: Berlin Station
items.whyPriority: EPIX. CIA officers in Berlin. Smaller-budget but solid Le Carré-tradition show that didn't break through.
items.pitch: CIA's Berlin station works to stop leaks and insider threats. Espionage as office work.

items.title: Counterpart
items.whyPriority: Cross-listed Sci-Fi TV. Spy drama with parallel-worlds premise. J.K. Simmons in two roles. Cancelled too early.
items.pitch: A UN bureaucrat in Berlin discovers his agency is the gateway between two parallel Earths that diverged 30 years ago. A version of him is a spy from the other Earth.

critics.quote: Cult devotion. J.K. Simmons gives one of the best dual performances on TV.

items.title: The Spy
items.pitch: Sacha Baron Cohen as Eli Cohen, the Mossad agent who infiltrated Syrian government circles in the 1960s. True story.

critics.quote: Cohen's first major dramatic role. Critically warm.

items.title: Homeland
items.seasons: S1-2 considered peak (loved); S3-7 declined; S8 recovered
items.pitch: Bipolar CIA officer believes a returning POW has been turned by Al-Qaeda. S1 is an all-time great season.

critics.quote: S1 won Outstanding Drama 2012. Show's reputation diminished across run but S1-S2 stand.

items.title: Rubicon
items.whyPriority: AMC. Slow-burn 70s-paranoia-style intelligence drama set at a Manhattan analytical institute. Cancelled because no one watched. Beloved by the few who did.
items.pitch: Will Travers analyzes intelligence at a private DC firm. He notices a pattern in the deaths of his colleagues. Things deepen.

critics.quote: Considered the spiritual heir to Three Days of the Condor. AMC pulled the plug too early.

items.title: MI-5 (Spooks)
items.whyPriority: BBC. Long-running British MI5 drama. Operations-focused. Hit characters routinely killed off — high stakes for genuinely beloved cast.
items.pitch: MI5 Section D. Each episode an operation; multi-episode arcs build to season-end crises.

critics.quote: Three BAFTAs for Best Drama. Cultural staple in the UK.

sections.name: IV. Foundational / Worth Considering
sections.desc: Older or smaller series that fit the lane.

items.title: I, Claudius
items.seasons: Limited series; one of the most acclaimed in TV history
items.pitch: BBC. Robert Graves novels of imperial Rome. Adjacent to spy because the Senate intrigue and palace conspiracies are the same mechanic.

critics.quote: Routinely listed among the greatest TV adaptations ever made. Set the template for prestige period drama.

items.title: Tehran
items.pitch: Apple TV+. Mossad operative inserted into Tehran. Shot largely in Athens for safety.

items.title: Fauda
items.pitch: IDF undercover unit operating in Palestinian territories. Created by two former operators. Politically polarizing depending on viewer's frame.

critics.quote: Critical and commercial international hit. Some Palestinian critics challenge the framing.

items.title: The Diplomat
items.pitch: Netflix. Keri Russell as US Ambassador to the UK during a crisis. West-Wing-tradition political procedural with espionage texture.

critics.quote: Cahn was an Americans writer. Critical favorite.

items.title: Le Carré: A Most Wanted Man (no series, but)
items.pitch: Several Le Carré novels have been adapted as feature films but not as series — A Most Wanted Man, A Perfect Spy (1987 BBC, 7-ep, also worth seeking), The Spy Who Came in from the Cold. Mentioned here for completeness.

items.title: A Perfect Spy
items.pitch: BBC adaptation of the Le Carré novel. Considered one of his most autobiographical books and an excellent adaptation.

critics.quote: BAFTA-nominated. Cult standing among Le Carré devotees.
